column1: # xlsx merged ranges paint the anchor's content across the merge's
# column span; non-anchor cells (and subsequent rows of multi-row
# merges) render blank.  Cursor navigation does NOT snap to the
# anchor for v1 — pointer can sit in non-anchor cells.
# Pointer starts on A1 — the anchor of the horizontal merge.  Move
# right so we can see the unhighlighted wide-paint of "Header"
# (highlighted anchor stays REVERSED but content is still readable;
# moving off makes the visual unambiguous).
# Anchor cell shows the content, non-anchor cells are blank.
# Cursor snap is *not* yet implemented — pointer can land in a
# non-anchor cell of the merge.  This pins the v1 behavior; flip the
# Rectangular merge B3:C4: anchor B3 shows "Box", the other three
# cells of the rectangle render blank.  A3 is outside the merge.
# Control: A5 is not merged, content visible normally.
